payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [FM-1.1] Disobey task specification (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Repair the planning artifact or traceability gap before invoking the lead again.
2. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=c360f372-1416-4a7f-b8df-3a90d2a0c572): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

claims: P1: run_durable_evaluator_trials routes live execution through reserve/claim/complete on dual_agent_workflow_jobs lane; test asserts idempotency_token, recovery_point==terminal, terminal_status==completed

P2: _load_progress keeps contiguous trial prefix (break on gap); trial loop range(len(metric_trials),k_trials); _write_progress per trial; crash-once test trial-0 count==1, trial-1 count==2, metric_trials==[0.7,0.75,0.8]

P3: post-trial budget check appends budget_exceeded+break; subprocess timeout=max(0.001,timeout_s); validation adds budget_exceeded+timeout gaming_flags; budget excluded from _retryable_evaluator_crash
P4: resolve_evaluator_defaults fills replay_corpus only when ref AND hash absent, sha256-pins local script, metric_name=pass_rate; test confirms ref resolves + metric_name==pass_rate
P5: default_change_allowed=False hardcoded in schema.py/report.py/validation.py/orchestrator.py unchanged by this work; test asserts validation default_change_allowed is False

criteria: Each PRD promise traces to a real source symbol and a named test
Forbidden outcomes are disprovable by at least one non-vacuous test (P2 crash-once counts, P3 limit flags)
Report-only invariants hardcoded False and unchanged by this work
evidence: ACCEPT prd_review for durable-evaluator-replay-corpus-20260610: all five promise contracts map to real tested public boundaries; grill findings 1-4 resolved; out-of-scope respected
confidence.rationale: All P1-P5 verified against current source by direct git diff + Read; resume and timeout tests confirmed non-vacuous. Held below 0.9 because pytest was not run (approval not given) and shasum was denied, so test_status and PRD-hash equality are self_reported rather than independently verified.

payload.content: ACCEPT. PRD for durable hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluator jobs + replay-corpus default evaluator. All five promise contracts (P1 durable job boundary, P2 trial-level resume, P3 budget/timeout limit flags, P4 replay-corpus default, P5 report-only invariants) map to real, tested public boundaries in current source. Grill findings 1-4 resolved and reflected in code. Out-of-scope respected. Strongest objection is a documentation NIT (evaluator-jobs/ artifact paths undocumented in Impl Decisions line 53). Residuals self_reported: pytest un-run, shasum denied.

assumptions_to_verify: PRD source/ copy is byte-identical to top-level copy and to handoff sha (assumed from prior read; shasum denied)
The 5 named tests pass under pytest (assumed GREEN from source read; not executed)
contradictions_checked: P5 invariant locations grep-confirmed hardcoded False and absent from this diff (no policy/gate mutation introduced)
resolve_evaluator_defaults gate (ref AND hash absent) matches PRD line 55 forbidden-default-override semantics
P2 resume test is non-vacuous: distinct trial counts across runs disprove rerun/lost/double-count
Out-of-scope items (dispatcher, gate sequence, new runtime, fan-out/reviewer defaults) absent from changed files
missing_evidence: pytest run output for the 5 named tests (approval not given; test_status unknown/self_reported)
shasum reconfirmation that PRD source/ copy and top-level copy match handoff sha d1442495 (shasum command denied; self_reported)
critical_review.strongest_objection: PRD Implementation Decisions line 53 documents evaluator-runs/<attempt>.progress.json and <attempt>.json but does not mention the evaluator-jobs/<attempt>/request|result|log artifacts that durable_jobs._job_paths actually writes — under-documentation of the durable job artifact layout, not a contradiction with any promise contract
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing pytest run on any of the 5 named tests, OR discovery that resolve_evaluator_defaults can override an explicitly supplied evaluator ref/hash, OR any diff hunk mutating policy/gate-advancement state — any of these would move the decision to revise or deny

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: source/issues.md defines 4 slices that cover all 5 PRD promises with no orphans
Slice 1 ACs realized: durable_jobs.py reserves/claims/completes one job row; test:173 asserts recovery_point=terminal and kind=autoresearch_evaluator non-vacuously
Slice 2 ACs realized: trial-level resume (test:435 proves trial-0 not rerun), budget_exceeded (test:501) and timeout (test:543) flags surfaced via validation.py:81-84
Slice 3 ACs realized: empty ref/hash resolves replay_corpus.py hash-pinned with computed pass_rate + IQR (test:586; resolve_evaluator_defaults:26-35)

P5 invariants default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced hardcoded False (validation.py:135-137) and asserted for live run (test:867); P1 live execution routes through run_durable_evaluator_trials (orchestrator.py:112)

criteria: PRD promises P1-P5 each map to >=1 slice (verified)
No orphan slices — all 4 slices trace to promises (verified)
Each slice AC backed by real source symbol (verified durable_jobs/evaluator/replay_corpus/orchestrator/validation)
Named TDD tests exist and are non-vacuous (verified bodies at :173/:435/:501/:543/:586/:867)
P5 report-only invariants hardcoded False and asserted (verified validation.py:135-137, test:867)
Full pytest suite green (NOT verified — execution denied, self_reported)
evidence: ACCEPT the issues_review gate: 4 slices exhaustively cover PRD P1-P5 with no orphan slices and every acceptance criterion is realizable and source+test backed
confidence.rationale: All 4 slices verified to map PRD P1-P5 with no orphans, and each acceptance criterion was confirmed against real source symbols (durable_jobs.py, evaluator.py, replay_corpus.py, orchestrator.py, validation.py) and non-vacuous test bodies. Confidence capped below 0.95 because pytest was not executed (denied), so Slice 4's pass-claims are self_reported, consistent with the lead_direct self_reported evidence grade.

payload.content: issues_review ACCEPT: source/issues.md 4 slices exhaustively cover PRD P1-P5 with no orphans; every acceptance criterion is source+test backed and non-vacuous. Slice1 durable job adapter (durable_jobs.py:64/83/210; test:173 asserts 1 row, recovery_point=terminal, kind=autoresearch_evaluator). Slice2 resume/budget/timeout (resume test:435 proves trial-0 not rerun; budget:501 budget_exceeded; timeout:543; validation.py:81-84 surfaces flags). Slice3 replay-corpus default (test:586 empty ref/hash resolves replay_corpus.py, hash-pinned, pass_rate, IQR; resolve_evaluator_defaults:26-35). Slice4 regression self_reported (pytest denied). P5 invariants validation.py:135-137 + test:867; P1 routing orchestrator.py:112.

assumptions_to_verify: Replay-corpus default test (:586) runs against repo_root=cwd and depends on the pinned agentic eval corpus being present and loadable at execution time
CI/full pytest actually passes green as Slice 4 AC asserts
contradictions_checked: Top-level issues.md is a ledger stub ('No issue artifacts recorded') but source/issues.md is the validated 4-slice artifact (sha fb70c090, planning event 651726) — resolved: gate subject is source/ copy, not stub
P1 forbids non-durable live execution — confirmed orchestrator.py:112 has no direct-call path; live always routes through run_durable_evaluator_trials
P5 forbids gate advancement — confirmed validation.py hardcodes the three invariants False and report_emitted payload sets default_change_allowed False
missing_evidence: pytest run output for the focused suites and full suite (denied)
shasum reconfirmation of the source/ planning artifacts against handoff sha values (denied)
critical_review.strongest_objection: As an issues_review gate, the question is whether the issue slices are well-formed and exhaustive, not whether the implementation passes. The issues.md ACs are written in spec-form and do not name the exact backing tests, and pytest was denied so Slice 4's 'full suite passes' AC cannot be verified — leaving one AC self_reported.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Discovery of a PRD promise with no covering slice, an AC with no backing test, a vacuous/tautological test body, or a live-execution path bypassing the durable job lane (violating P1) would move this to REVISE or DENY.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

assumptions_to_verify: The pytest receipts were produced after the final implementation edits and are not stale.
The production Postgres state path observes the same reserve, claim, complete semantics as the inspected SQLite-backed tests.
The intended P5 contract allows gate_advanced to be record-level rather than requiring a top-level report field.
contradictions_checked: Source artifact hashes matched supplied receipt hashes.
All six named tests were found at the cited lines.
All six named tests call run_autoresearch_fixture at the public boundary.
git diff --name-only only lists four tracked modified files, but git ls-files/status show the two new untracked implementation files; this reconciles the declared six-file surface.
The broader worktree has unrelated untracked docs/scratch files, so the no-orphans claim is only valid when scoped to implementation files.
Report-only validation code hardcodes default_change_allowed=false, policy_mutated=false, and gate_advanced=false for records; report summary lacks gate_advanced but does not set it true.
missing_evidence: Independent pytest execution by this reviewer within this gate.
Observed RED-first failures before implementation.
Explicit report-level gate_advanced=false assertion or a documented decision that gate_advanced is record-only.
AutoResearch durable evaluator run against a live Postgres-backed State.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

claims: 6 plan-declared files map 1:1 to git changes (4 modified, 2 new) with no code orphans

resolve_evaluator_defaults returns experiment unchanged when evaluator_ref OR evaluator_hash present, else pins supervisor/autoresearch/evaluators/replay_corpus.py with sha256 and metric_name=pass_rate (durable_jobs.py:20-30)
idempotency token format autoresearch:<run>:<exp>:<attempt> implemented at durable_jobs.py:369 and asserted exactly at tests/test_autoresearch.py:209
P2 resume test asserts trial-0 counter==1 and trial-1 counter==2, proving completed trials are not rerun (tests:493-498)
P3 budget test rejects with budget_exceeded flag (:501); P3 timeout test rejects with timeout flag (:543)
P4 default-evaluator test asserts metric_name==pass_rate, metric_source==evaluator_execution, metric_iqr not None (:586)
P5 live run asserts default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced all False (:900-905)
validation.py adds budget_exceeded and timeout gaming flags while preserving outside_mutable_surface/hash/mutable-path checks
evaluator.py uses exclude_roots=(output_dir,) so progress/job artifacts do not trip the source-mutation detector

criteria: file ownership matches git changes exactly with no code orphans
every declared Step maps to a real symbol in the implemented code
each named traceability test exists verbatim and is non-vacuous
P5 report-only invariants asserted false on a live run
artifact sha256 reconfirmed against handoff (NOT met: shasum DENIED, self_reported)
confidence.rationale: All structural checks pass against current source: file ownership EXACT, steps map to real symbols, 6 named tests exist verbatim at declared lines with strong non-vacuous assertions (exact idempotency token, exact resume counters, budget/timeout flags, computed pass_rate, report-only invariants false). Confidence capped below 0.95 because pytest was not executed and artifact shasum verification was DENIED by the report-only lead constraint, so test pass/fail and artifact-hash integrity are self_reported per the handoff evidence grade.

payload.content: Implementation plan accurately reflects implemented code: 6 declared files map 1:1 to git changes with no code orphans, Steps 1-5 trace to real symbols, and 6 named traceability tests exist verbatim and non-vacuously cover P1-P5 (durable job row+exact idempotency token, trial-level resume with counters proving trial-0 not rerun, budget+timeout flags, replay-corpus default computing pass_rate, report-only invariants false on live run). Accept and advance.

Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.

Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

assumptions_to_verify: Whether timeout_s is intended as a hard per-subprocess limit whose failure must terminally reject the attempt
Whether any production dispatcher bounds retryable evaluator crashes outside this adapter
Whether top-level skill receipt artifact paths are display aliases while source/* paths are canonical for hash validation
Whether the receipt-backed pytest runs used the exact current worktree state
contradictions_checked: Implementation plan P3 says budget_usd and timeout_s overrun attempts are rejected with machine-readable limit flags
Implementation plan risk says timeout and crash handling must not retry forever
tests/test_autoresearch.py timeout test uses k_trials=1, so timeout occurs before any metric_trials and does not cover the partial-progress branch
durable_jobs.py retry classification excludes budget_exceeded but not timeout, making partial timeout retryable
source/* artifact hashes match P_planning accepted hashes; top-level files are gate transcripts, not the canonical source artifacts
missing_evidence: A test where trial 0 completes, trial 1 times out, and the durable job reaches terminal failed/rejected state with timeout gaming flag
A bounded retry policy if timeout is intentionally retryable rather than a limit failure
Fresh pytest execution in this reviewer session; current passed status is receipt-backed, not re-run here

Unambiguous receipt artifact paths for source/* planning artifacts; top-level exported gate transcript files have different hashes than the skill receipt hashes, though the validator uses source/* paths
critical_review.strongest_objection: P3 is not durably satisfied for timeout after partial progress. run_evaluator_trials only raises when execution_errors exist and metric_trials is empty; with one completed trial it returns a timeout error plus partial metrics. durable_jobs._retryable_evaluator_crash then treats any timeout error as retryable while len(metric_trials) < k_trials, requeues the job to request_written, and raises retryable_evaluator_crash. That can repeat indefinitely and leaves the durable job non-terminal instead of producing a terminal rejected limit outcome.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Advance if timeout limit failures are made terminal and rejected even after completed trial progress, with a regression test for partial-progress timeout, or if the product explicitly defines timeout as bounded retryable crash and adds the bounded retry mechanism plus evidence.

Implementation plan risk says timeout and crash handling must not retry forever

Unambiguous receipt artifact paths for source/* planning artifacts; top-level exported gate transcript files have different hashes than the skill receipt hashes, though the validator uses source/* paths
critical_review.strongest_objection: P3 is not durably satisfied for timeout after partial progress. run_evaluator_trials only raises when execution_errors exist and metric_trials is empty; with one completed trial it returns a timeout error plus partial metrics. durable_jobs._retryable_evaluator_crash then treats any timeout error as retryable while len(metric_trials) < k_trials, requeues the job to request_written, and raises retryable_evaluator_crash. That can repeat indefinitely and leaves the durable job non-terminal instead of producing a terminal rejected limit outcome.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Advance if timeout limit failures are made terminal and rejected even after completed trial progress, with a regression test for partial-progress timeout, or if the product explicitly defines timeout as bounded retryable crash and adds the bounded retry mechanism plus evidence.

strongest_objection.text: P3 is not durably satisfied for timeout after partial progress. run_evaluator_trials only raises when execution_errors exist and metric_trials is empty; with one completed trial it returns a timeout error plus partial metrics. durable_jobs._retryable_evaluator_crash then treats any timeout error as retryable while len(metric_trials) < k_trials, requeues the job to request_written, and raises retryable_evaluator_crash. That can repeat indefinitely and leaves the durable job non-terminal instead of producing a terminal rejected limit outcome.

That can repeat indefinitely and leaves the durable job non-terminal instead of producing a terminal rejected limit outcome.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Advance if timeout limit failures are made terminal and rejected even after completed trial progress, with a regression test for partial-progress timeout, or if the product explicitly defines timeout as bounded retryable crash and adds the bounded retry mechanism plus evidence.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
independent_reviewer_blocking_objection: independent-reviewer-1

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=cocreate-global-checkpoint-purge-20260610): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=cocreate-global-checkpoint-purge-20260610): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

claims: P1: run_durable_evaluator_trials creates and completes one durable job row with an autoresearch_evaluator request payload and stable idempotency token autoresearch:<run_id>:<experiment_id>:<attempt_id> (test at line 173).
P2: per-trial progress is persisted and reloaded so completed trials are not rerun on retry (crash-resume test at line 435 asserts trial-0 executed once, trial-1 twice).
P3: budget overrun and timeout map to budget_exceeded/timeout gaming flags and reject the attempt; partial-progress timeout is terminal with no infinite requeue (tests at lines 501, 543, 586).
P4: empty evaluator_ref+hash resolves the hash-pinned local replay_corpus.py with metric_name pass_rate (test at line 656).
P5: default_change_allowed, policy_mutated, gate_advanced remain False for live runs (test at line 937).
test_status is unknown because pytest approval was denied; these claims are self_reported from static verification, not an executed receipt.

criteria: All 6 plan files present in worktree (met)
All 7 TDD-named tests present, non-vacuous, public-boundary (met by static read)
Executed green test receipt for focused+full suite (NOT met: pytest denied)
Report-only invariants asserted False (met by static read of test line 937)
confidence.rationale: Implementation is complete, coherent, and the 7 tests are non-vacuous and map every PRD promise, with a non-empty whitespace-clean diff. Confidence is held below 0.80 solely because no test was actually executed (pytest denied), so the GREEN state is inferred, not observed, and the diff was not authored this session.

payload.content: Durable budget-enforced AutoResearch evaluator jobs plus a hash-pinned replay-corpus default evaluator are implemented across evaluator.py, orchestrator.py, validation.py, durable_jobs.py and evaluators/replay_corpus.py, with 7 non-vacuous public-boundary tests mapping P1-P5. Deliverable diff present (463 insertions, git diff --check clean). Static verification complete; test execution could not be run because pytest approval was denied, so no green receipt is cited and test_status is unknown.

assumptions_to_verify: That the seven tests actually pass when executed (currently assumed from static structure).
That resolve_evaluator_defaults only fills defaults when BOTH evaluator_ref and evaluator_hash are empty (read as such, not executed).
That the durable job idempotency token reattaches correctly under a real crash/retry rather than only in the simulated SystemExit fixture.
contradictions_checked: Checked that report-only invariants are not silently flipped: test line 937 asserts default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced all False — no contradiction.
Checked FM-1.5 termination: partial-progress timeout test (line 586) asserts terminal_status==failed and trial counts 1/1, proving no infinite requeue.
Checked FM-1.3 repetition: did not re-submit the same denied probe blindly; switched evidence to git diffstat/--check after recognizing the denial pattern.
missing_evidence: pytest pass/fail output for the focused suite and full suite
Confirmation that the tests were ever observed failing before implementation (RED phase)
SHA reconfirmation of the hash-pinned replay_corpus.py at execution time
critical_review.strongest_objection: There is no executed test receipt: pytest was denied three times, so I cannot prove RED-then-GREEN was actually observed; I am accepting on static inspection of a pre-existing diff, which risks rubber-stamping an unverified GREEN.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: An executed pytest run showing any of the 7 tests failing or vacuous, or evidence that the replay-corpus default can spend live model budget or mutate policy, would flip this to revise/deny.

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Run hash-pinned AutoResearch evaluators as durable budget-enforced jobs and ship a replay-corpus reference evaluator as the default metric source.

assumptions_to_verify: Current working-tree source equals what the workflow job actually executed (no post-submit drift).
The 7 tests actually pass under the real venv (asserted green by handoff, unverified here).
Planning-artifact contents match the sha256 hashes pinned in the handoff packet.
contradictions_checked: durable_jobs.py :357 timeout branch appears dead because :355 short-circuits timeouts first - confirmed defensive, not a contradiction.

evaluator.py :196 raises only when execution_errors and not metric_trials, so partial-progress timeout returns with errors rather than raising - consistent with terminal-but-reported behavior, not a contradiction.
Report-only invariants hardcoded False in validation.py vs. intent that AutoResearch cannot mutate policy/advance gates - consistent.
missing_evidence: Supervisor poll_dual_agent_workflow_job(job_id='workflow-c59f17b7c01e') output confirming a terminal job row.
Live pytest pass/fail for tests/test_autoresearch.py and the full suite.
shasum verification that the five planning artifacts match the handoff sha256 values.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The gate accepts without any independent execution evidence: the supervisor poll (workflow-c59f17b7c01e), the focused and full pytest runs, and the planning-artifact shasum checks are all approval-blocked, so the durable job row and the green test suite are asserted at self_reported grade and never confirmed at runtime in this environment.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing pytest run, a poll result showing a non-terminal or missing job row, a shasum mismatch on any planning artifact, or evidence that the partial-progress timeout can requeue (contradicting test :586) would flip this to REVISE or DENY.

Independent runtime evidence (poll/pytest/shasum): NOT met (approval-blocked, self_reported per handoff)
claude_outcome.confidence_rationale: Static source and test verification is complete and clean: all six files trace to P1-P5, file ownership matches the plan, and the two termination/resume tests are non-vacuous, directly disproving FM-1.5. Confidence is held at 0.8 (not higher) because no independent execution evidence could be produced - the supervisor poll, pytest, and shasum are all approval-blocked, so green tests and the live job row are asserted at self_reported grade rather than verified at runtime.

assumptions_to_verify: Current worktree source is what execution gate tested (no post-execution drift).
P4 test coupling to Path.cwd() remains acceptable for default replay-corpus evaluator.
SQLite-local job ledger tests adequately proxy production Postgres SKIP-LOCKED semantics.
contradictions_checked: Prior FM-1.5 claim that partial-timeout is retryable — disproved: durable_jobs.py :355 excludes timeout before :357 retry branch.
durable_jobs.py :357 timeout sub-check is dead after :355 — defensive only, not a functional bug.
evaluator.py :196 raises only when errors and no metric_trials — partial-timeout returns errors+metrics but validation rejects via timeout flag; consistent.
Claude test_status unknown vs Cursor independent run — resolved: all 7 pass.
missing_evidence: Git commit bundling the two new untracked source files (durable_jobs.py, evaluators/replay_corpus.py).
Supervisor poll_dual_agent_workflow_job output for workflow-c59f17b7c01e in this review session.
Full-suite pytest re-run in this session (only the 7 named tests were executed).
critical_review.strongest_objection: Deliverables remain uncommitted and the worktree contains ~72 unrelated changed files, so the reviewed source may diverge from what eventually ships even though tests pass on the current tree.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Failing pytest on any of the 7 tests, shasum mismatch on planning artifacts, evidence that partial-timeout requeues (trial counts increment on retry), or missing/incorrect durable job terminal row in a live supervisor poll.

assumptions_to_verify: Runtime receipts were produced after the final implementation edits and against the same worktree state
Production Postgres reserve/claim/complete behavior matches the inspected ledger adapter expectations
Consumers treat durable job terminal_status and validation_status as authoritative when an event payload says execution_job_completed with execution_errors
contradictions_checked: Earlier outcome_review blocked event for screenshots was superseded by later accepted user_facing=false artifact-rigor state where screenshots are not required
Claude's approval-blocked shasum/pytest objection is contradicted by supplied runtime receipts and local shasum verification, but this strengthens rather than weakens acceptance
Worktree has unrelated untracked docs/scratch files; scoped implementation deliverables still match the six declared code/test files
Partial-progress timeout returns an execution with errors, produces terminal_status=failed, and does not requeue in the inspected test
Validation sets report-only fields false at public validate_attempt outputs; schema could serialize true only if bypassing that public boundary
missing_evidence: Independent pytest rerun by this reviewer in the current sandbox
Direct supervisor poll output for workflow-c59f17b7c01e
Explicit top-level report gate_advanced=false assertion or documented decision that gate_advanced is record-only
Live Postgres-backed durable evaluator exercise beyond SQLite/public-boundary tests
critical_review.strongest_objection: The best reason not to advance is residual evidence/coverage looseness: this read-only pass did not rerun pytest or directly poll workflow-c59f17b7c01e, and P5 is asserted on validation records while the top-level report lacks an explicit gate_advanced=false field. That is not enough to block because receipts and source inspection cover the promised public behavior.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: I would revise or deny if pytest fails on this tree, the workflow poll shows a missing/nonterminal job row, any pinned artifact hash mismatches, Postgres job semantics diverge from the tests, or P5 is clarified to require an explicit top-level report gate_advanced=false field.

objections: Deliverables are still uncommitted (durable_jobs.py, evaluators/replay_corpus.py untracked); 72-file worktree churn includes unrelated docs—process isolation risk, not a functional failure found.
